chunk_summary: This section discusses screening and staged follow-up for obesity comorbidities in children and adolescents exposed to ultra-processed foods, emphasizing clinical guidelines and tailored interventions based on risk factors and severity.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of ultra-processed food exposure in obesity-related comorbidities.
Learn the IAP childhood obesity guideline for screening and follow-up.
Identify appropriate laboratory evaluations and staged clinical interventions for obese children.
Explore counseling strategies for adolescents addressing environmental and behavioral factors.
key_facts: High exposure to ultra-processed foods is linked to cardiometabolic risks requiring staged clinical evaluation.
Screen all obese children aged 10+ for hypertension, dyslipidemia, hyperglycemia, and MASLD; screen overweight children aged 10+ with risk factors.
Routine endocrine testing is not recommended unless obesity is accompanied by short stature or diagnostic clues.
Laboratory evaluations include fasting lipid profile, ALT for MASLD/NAFLD, renal function, fasting glucose, and HbA1c or OGTT when indicated.
Follow-up stages: Stage 1 (4–6 weeks), Stage 2 (6–12 weeks), Stage 3 (refer to dietitian/endocrinology for severe cases).
reference_quote: Use the IAP childhood obesity guideline as the clinical anchor for screening. (Indian Academy of Pediatrics, 2023)
document_key_topics: Ultra-processed food exposure and obesity risk
Obesity comorbidity screening in children and adolescents
